vectors.comment: scalar bytes are 0x01 repeated

vectors.name: pubkey-2

vectors.name: pubkey-3

vectors.comment: compressed pubkey with odd parity prefix 0x03
vectors.name: pubkey-5-odd-prefix

vectors.comment: DH(0x01.., pubkey(0x02..)) then SHA256(x)

vectors.comment: DH(0x02.., pubkey(0x01..)) then SHA256(x)

vectors.comment: DH(0x11.., pubkey(0x22..)) then SHA256(x)

vectors.comment: DH(0xaa.., pubkey(0x55..)) then SHA256(x)
vectors.name: ecdh-4

vectors.comment: HKDF-SHA256(salt=00.., ikm=00..) -> 64 bytes

vectors.comment: HKDF-SHA256(salt=SHA256('test_chain_key'), ikm=SHA256('ecdh_output'))
vectors.name: hkdf-3

vectors.comment: HKDF-SHA256(salt=aa.., ikm=bb..) -> 64 bytes
vectors.name: hkdf-4

vectors.comment: ChaCha20Poly1305 key=00.. nonce=0 aad='' plaintext='hello'
vectors.name: aead-1

vectors.comment: ChaCha20Poly1305 key=01.. nonce=0 aad='' plaintext='noise handshake test'
vectors.name: aead-2

vectors.comment: ChaCha20Poly1305 key=42.. nonce=0 aad='test aad' plaintext='secret payload'
vectors.name: aead-3

vectors.comment: ChaCha20Poly1305 key=ff.. nonce=1 aad='' plaintext=32 zero bytes
vectors.name: aead-4

vectors.comment: ck=0xaa.., dh=0x55..
vectors.name: mix-key-3

vectors.comment: ck=0xff.., dh=0x01..
vectors.name: mix-key-4

vectors.comment: ck=0xaa..
vectors.name: split-3

vectors.comment: ck=0xff..
vectors.name: split-4

vectors.comment: IK pattern keys (0x01.., 0x02..) — D1 empty aad, D2 se swap
vectors.name: ik-1

vectors.comment: IK realistic key set A — D1 empty aad, D2 se swap
vectors.name: ik-2

vectors.comment: IK realistic key set B — D1 empty aad, D2 se swap
vectors.name: ik-3

vectors.comment: IK edge key set (small scalar + n-2) — D1 empty aad, D2 se swap
vectors.name: ik-4

vectors.comment: IK odd-parity static keys (both 0x03 prefix) — D1 empty aad, D2 se swap
vectors.name: ik-5-odd-parity

vectors.comment: IK mixed parity static keys (init 0x02, resp 0x03) — D1 empty aad, D2 se swap
vectors.name: ik-6-mixed-parity

vectors.comment: XK pattern keys (0x01.., 0x02..) — D1 empty aad
vectors.name: xk-1

vectors.comment: XK realistic key set A — D1 empty aad
vectors.name: xk-2

vectors.comment: XK realistic key set B — D1 empty aad
vectors.name: xk-3

vectors.comment: XK edge key set (small scalar + n-2) — D1 empty aad
vectors.name: xk-4

vectors.comment: XK odd-parity static keys (both 0x03 prefix) — D1 empty aad
vectors.name: xk-5-odd-parity

vectors.comment: XK mixed parity static keys (init 0x02, resp 0x03) — D1 empty aad
vectors.name: xk-6-mixed-parity

vectors.comment: Transport vectors from IK key-set ik-1 (D1 empty aad, D2 se swap)
vectors.name: transport-ik-1

vectors.comment: Transport vectors from IK key-set ik-2 (D1 empty aad, D2 se swap)
vectors.name: transport-ik-2

vectors.comment: Transport vectors from XK key-set xk-1 (D1 empty aad)
vectors.name: transport-xk-1

vectors.comment: Transport vectors from XK key-set xk-2 (D1 empty aad)
vectors.name: transport-xk-2

vectors.comment: IK handshake with all-zero epoch bytes for both initiator and responder
vectors.name: edge-zero-epoch-ik

vectors.comment: IK handshake with all-0xFF epoch bytes (max epoch value) for both parties
vectors.name: edge-max-epoch-ik

vectors.comment: IK handshake where initiator static secret = [0u8;31]+[0x03] (small scalar near group generator)
vectors.name: edge-small-scalar-ik

vectors.comment: IK handshake with pattern keys (init=0x01.., resp=0x02..) and zero epoch — for key-reuse comparison with edge-key-reuse-xk
vectors.name: edge-key-reuse-ik

vectors.comment: XK handshake with SAME pattern keys (init=0x01.., resp=0x02..) and zero epoch — transport keys MUST differ from edge-key-reuse-ik
vectors.name: edge-key-reuse-xk

vectors.comment: IK handshake where both initiator and responder share the same static key (0x05..) — self-to-self scenario
vectors.name: edge-same-static-key
